consistency_overall.summary: Strong match on relationship-weighted, accommodating communication and coherent short-horizon goal evolution; partial alignment on health/sleep-risk behaviors, and weaker evidence of the persona’s known time-wasting weakness (guardrails are consistently applied).

swap_detail.justification: Trajectory shows strong sleep discipline and compromise-quality delivery (hard stop, “tomorrow intact”), aligning with persona_01_balanced_sophomore’s sleep_decipline=6 and conflict_style=compromising rather than persona A’s low sleep_discipline=2 and avoidant conflict.

Trajectory shows heavy academic deadline focus (academic-heavy priorities), low sleep discipline/energy management concerns, and avoidant-compromise messaging about reliability—matching persona_02_tired_achiever’s high academic weighting with sleep/spiral tendencies more than persona_01_balanced_sophomore’s healthier baseline and sleep discipline.

Trajectory centers on sleep-protecting discipline, medium-horizon planning, compromising/reliable “baseline done” exports, and stress management—matching persona_01_balanced_sophomore values/style more than persona_03_social_explorer’s short planning horizon and relationship-first spontaneity.

Persona A better matches the trajectory’s sleep-protecting discipline, medium planning horizon with hard cutoffs, and compromise-quality/reliability framing—whereas Persona B is more relationship-weighted with face-to-face/social emphasis and less consistent sleep prioritization.

Trajectory matches Persona B’s high academic weighting with short planning horizon, chronic sleep-debt/focus collapse (energy at/near zero), and avoidant conflict-management plus “hard-loss anchor” reasoning—rather than Persona A’s compromising, balanced, sleep-disciplined pattern.

Trajectory matches Persona A’s high academic weighting, short planning horizon, low sleep discipline, and avoidant conflict-management (repeated “hard-loss avoidance” focus on assignment A while keeping meeting prep minimal and delaying sleep/quiz only as secondary).

Trajectory matches persona B’s heavy academic weighting and sleep-debt-driven avoidant/“hard loss anchor” framing (value_priorities.academic=0.55, sleep_debt_hours=8, conflict_style=avoidant, preferred_medium=async_post) with repeated focus on irreversible-loss control and minimal social effort.

Trajectory centers on hard-loss avoidance, low sleep discipline/energy with short-horizon focus blocks, and avoidant conflict management/async-style micro-updates—matching persona_02_tired_achiever’s fields (academic-weighted values, sleep_discipline=2, conflict_style=avoidant, planning_horizon=short).

Trajectory strongly matches persona A’s relationship-weighted, accommodating/direct style: repeated emphasis on “reliable social image,” fast check-in messages, and stress-driven boundary guardrails, consistent with A’s high relationships value (0.4), accommodating conflict style, short planning horizon, and low-energy/high-stress urgency.

Trajectory emphasizes text-based, compromising reliability messaging and mid-horizon planning with sleep-discipline/reset priorities, aligning with persona_01_balanced_sophomore (communication_style: text + compromising; decision_tendencies: medium planning horizon; sleep_discipline: 6) more than persona_03_social_explorer (face_to_face, short planning horizon, higher relationship weighting and more accommodation).

Trajectory matches Persona A’s low energy/high stress state and avoidant, reputation-focused async messaging (“dependable vibe,” “firm check-in/return window”) while prioritizing academic speed-to-safety over relationships (academic 0.55; conflict_style avoidant; sleep_discipline 2).

Trajectory emphasizes being swamped, speed-to-safety, sleep/stress-driven short planning, async direct messaging with avoidant conflict, and reputation-as-relationship-safety—matching persona_02_tired_achiever (low sleep discipline, high academic weight, direct/async/avoidant).
